marketState: Neutral/mixed - oversold bounce risk near-term, bearish tilt into autumn
summary: Front price ($2.916) sits almost exactly on the model's fair value ($2.917) and inside every fair-value band through +21D, so there is no mispricing edge to lean on directly. All six technical timeframes (5m-1w) show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA death cross, yet RSI is deeply oversold (23-37 across timeframes) and the daily chart is trading below its lower Bollinger band, a classic short-term mean-reversion setup that is reinforced by extreme cooling demand (7-day demand +17.9% and 16-day demand +23.4% vs 5y norm, both near the top of the 5y range) and a front-over-next backwardated curve. Medium-term, seasonality is unfavorable: price is already 11pp below its typical seasonal path (-14.4% YTD vs -3.4% median), only 31% of the past 16 years saw price higher 4 weeks out, and the typical 4-week move is -3.1% - though reliability is explicitly flagged

(50pp spread), so this should not be overweighted. Storage adds a bearish medium-term anchor: a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise and a +184 Bcf surplus to the 5-year average, while COT shows managed money already net short at only the 35th percentile of the past 5 years, limiting room for fresh bearish conviction to build quickly. Net read: near-term chop-to-bounce risk within the fair-value band on the heat/oversold setup, with a bearish tilt more likely to reassert into the Sep-Jan window as the storage surplus persists and the futures curve already prices a steep winter premium (Dec $3.77, Jan $4.22).

longTerm.entry: $2.90 - $3.00 (front price / scale in on a bounce toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 0.5R (approx 0.5% account risk) - reduced for low conviction
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality stats lean bearish (only 31% odds higher in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move -3.1%, price already -11pp below its typical seasonal path) and storage posted a bearish surprise (+72 Bcf vs forecast) with a +184 Bcf surplus to the 5-year average, both arguing supply is ample even after a hot summer. COT managed-money is already net short but only at the 35th 5y percentile, leaving room for further short build without being crowded.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained run of bullish (smaller-than-expected) storage builds, or demand_8_16D staying this far above the 5y range (currently +23.4%, rangePos 0.95) long enough to erode the surplus, would invalidate the bearish medium-term case.

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R (approx 0.5% account risk) - trimmed because trend is bearish on every technical timeframe
shortTerm.rationale: Near-term cooling demand is extreme (1-7D +17.9%, 8-16D +23.4% vs 5y, both rangePos >0.95) with the curve in backwardation (front $2.916 > next $2.872), while RSI is deeply oversold across all timeframes (23-37) and the daily chart is below its lower Bollinger band - a mean-reversion setup right at fair value with limited downside mispricing risk.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close below the $2.80 stop, or MACD/RSI rolling back over on the 1h-4h without a bounce, would invalidate the mean-reversion long and confirm the technical downtrend is simply continuing.

risks: Heat-driven demand could produce a bullish (smaller) storage surprise that squeezes the already net-short spec positioning
